channel.title: Sintra Tesseract — AI News
channel.description: Curated AI news: model releases, benchmarks, research breakthroughs, and industry events.

item.title: Anthropic Files Confidential S-1 with SEC, Signalling IPO Path
item.description: Anthropic submitted a confidential draft S-1 registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, the standard first step toward a public offering. The company has not set a timeline or target price, and the filing is subject to SEC review. Analysts estimate a potential IPO valuation in the $80–100 billion range based on recent secondary-market transactions and the Series H price.

item.title: Microsoft Unveils MAI-Thinking-1 Reasoning Model at Build 2026
item.description: At Microsoft Build 2026, Microsoft Research introduced MAI-Thinking-1, the company's first internally developed frontier reasoning model. The model scores 97.4% on the AIME 2025 mathematics benchmark and 87.2% on GPQA-Diamond, placing it among the top-tier reasoning models globally. MAI-Thinking-1 will be available through Azure AI Foundry and integrated into Microsoft 365 Copilot Wave 4 later in 2026.

item.title: Project Glasswing: Anthropic's Automated Security Scanner Reaches 150 Organisations
item.description: Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing — its agentic vulnerability-discovery platform — to 150 participating organisations, up from the initial 10 during the May 2026 beta. The system uses Claude-based agents to autonomously identify critical and high-severity vulnerabilities in production codebases and generates remediation patches for human review. Early results show a median time-to-detection of 11 minutes for critical flaws versus 47 days using traditional scanners.

item.title: NVIDIA Open-Sources Cosmos 3 Omni-Model for Physical AI and Robotics
item.description: NVIDIA released Cosmos 3, a multimodal foundation model combining vision, language, and physics simulation for robotics and autonomous systems. Released under the NVIDIA Open Model License, Cosmos 3 can generate photorealistic synthetic training environments, predict object dynamics, and control robot arms with sub-millimetre precision in simulation. It ships with integrations for Isaac Sim, ROS 2, and OpenUSD.

item.title: NVIDIA RTX Spark: 128 GB Unified-Memory AI PC Unveiled at Computex 2026
item.description: NVIDIA announced RTX Spark, a palm-sized AI workstation superchip with 128 GB of unified LPDDR5X memory accessible to both the CPU and GPU at 900 GB/s bandwidth. The device runs 70-billion-parameter LLMs locally at 40+ tokens per second and ships with NVIDIA AI Enterprise pre-installed. Priced at $2,999, it targets enterprise developers who need on-device inference without cloud latency or data-privacy concerns.

item.title: OpenAI Announces GPT-4.5 Retirement on June 27, 2026
item.description: OpenAI issued a deprecation notice for GPT-4.5, its experimental large-parameter non-reasoning model released in February 2025. The model will be removed from the API on June 27, 2026. OpenAI cited high inference cost relative to newer models and low adoption compared to GPT-4.1 and the o-series as the primary reasons. Existing callers are advised to migrate to GPT-4.1 or o3 depending on their latency and capability requirements.

item.title: Sweden's Arrhenius AI Supercomputer Enters Full Production at NAISS
item.description: The Arrhenius AI supercomputer, operated by the National Academic Infrastructure for Supercomputing in Sweden (NAISS) at KTH Royal Institute of Technology, entered full production capacity with 4,096 NVIDIA H100 GPUs delivering 16 exaFLOPS of AI compute. The system is the largest academic AI supercomputer in the Nordic region and is allocated via the Swedish Research Council to universities and research institutions across Sweden.

item.title: Microsoft Completes $3 B AI Datacenter Expansion in Sweden, Bringing Total to 200k+ Servers
item.description: Microsoft announced the completion of its $3 billion AI datacenter expansion programme in Sweden, adding two new halls at the Gävle campus running entirely on renewable hydropower. The expansion brings Microsoft's total Sweden server count to over 200,000, making it the hyperscaler's largest European AI datacenter footprint. The facility will serve Azure AI customers across EMEA with data residency in the EU.

item.title: Brazil's Chamber of Deputies Schedules Full Vote on AI Framework Law for June 2026
item.description: Brazil's Câmara dos Deputados scheduled a floor vote on the Marco Legal da Inteligência Artificial (PL 2338/2023) for June 2026 after the special committee approved an amended text in May. The current draft establishes a risk-tier classification system similar to the EU AI Act, mandatory human oversight for high-risk AI in public services, and a National AI Authority. Brazil would become the first major Latin American country with a comprehensive AI law.

item.title: TCU Hosts Brazil AI Experience 2026 — Largest Public-Sector AI Conference in Latin America
item.description: Brazil's Federal Court of Accounts (TCU) hosted the AI Experience 2026 conference in Brasília with 3,200 attendees from federal, state, and municipal government agencies. The event featured demonstrations of AI tools deployed in public auditing, social programme fraud detection, and judicial case management. TCU announced a national programme to certify 50,000 public servants in AI literacy by end of 2027.

item.title: Snowflake and Anthropic: Claude Runs Inside Cortex AI — No Data Egress, Full Governance
item.description: At Snowflake Summit 26 in San Francisco, Snowflake and Anthropic detailed the technical architecture of their $200M partnership: Claude models run inside Snowflake Cortex AI natively, processing customer data without egress, fully governed by Snowflake's row- and column-level security policies. Enterprises can invoke Claude via SQL or Python directly in Snowflake Notebooks. The integration addresses the #1 blocker to enterprise AI adoption in financial services, healthcare, and insurance.

item.title: Salesforce Signs Agreement to Acquire Contentful for ~$1.5B to Power Agentforce AI Content
item.description: Salesforce signed a definitive agreement to acquire Contentful, a composable content management platform trusted by over 4,800 enterprises. The deal gives Agentforce a native content layer capable of delivering AI-assembled, personalised digital experiences at scale. The transaction is expected to close in Salesforce's fiscal Q3 2027, pending regulatory approval.

item.title: Alibaba Launches Qwen3.7-Plus — Multimodal GUI Agent Unifying Vision and Language
item.description: Alibaba released Qwen3.7-Plus, a multimodal agent model that unifies GUI and CLI operation across visual and text tasks in a single set of weights. On ScreenSpot Pro it scores 79.0% and Terminal-Bench 70.3%, leading the open-API GUI agent field. Available via Alibaba Cloud at $0.40/M input tokens with native vision for images and video up to 5 minutes.

item.title: GitHub Copilot Switches to Token-Based Billing — Developers Report Bills Up to 20× Higher
item.description: GitHub migrated all Copilot plans to usage-based token billing on June 1, replacing per-request pricing with metered AI credit consumption. Each plan includes a monthly AI credit allotment ($10 for Pro, $39 for Pro+), with additional usage billed at model API rates. Code completions remain unlimited, but agentic features using frontier models triggered reports of bills jumping from $29/month to $750 or more, sparking developer backlash across Reddit, X, and GitHub forums.

item.title: Microsoft Reveals Scout — 'Autopilot' Always-On AI Agent for Microsoft 365
item.description: Microsoft unveiled Scout at Build 2026, its first 'Autopilot' category AI agent: an always-on, identity-bearing assistant that operates continuously across Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint without being explicitly prompted. Scout handles meeting prep, scheduling conflicts, and routine tasks autonomously within user-set permissions. Initial access is in private preview through Microsoft's Frontier programme.

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026: Windows Repositioned as Agent Execution OS — Agent Runtime, IQ Grounding Layer
item.description: At Build 2026, Satya Nadella repositioned Windows as an agent execution platform with Windows Agent Framework, Microsoft Execution Containers, Intelligent Terminal, and Windows 365 for Agents. Microsoft IQ went generally available, providing four grounding layers — Web IQ, Work IQ, Foundry IQ, and Fabric IQ — enabling agents to query all four sources in a single inference call. GitHub Copilot Desktop with parallel sessions via git worktrees was released as part of the platform vision.

item.title: Salesforce Summer '26: Multi-Agent Orchestration and Agentforce Operations Generally Available
item.description: Salesforce's Summer 2026 release delivers Multi-Agent Orchestration enabling agents to collaborate as a unified team with shared context across all CRM channels, an Agentforce Self-Service Portal with AI-native UI, and Agentforce Operations targeting end-to-end back-office automation (AP, AR, procurement, HR onboarding). Tableau MCP integration allows AI agents to query analytics directly. Features are generally available from June 2–15, 2026.

item.title: EU Proposes Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA) — Triple EU Datacenter Capacity, Cloud Sovereignty Framework
item.description: The European Commission presented the Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA), targeting tripling EU datacenter capacity within 5–7 years to fully meet EU enterprise demand by 2035. CADA introduces a 4-tier EU Cloud Sovereignty Framework classifying cloud services by supply chain control, data residency, and infrastructure location. Commission President von der Leyen stated: 'We want to be sure nobody has a kill switch.' The package also includes Chips Act 2.0 and an Open Source Strategy.

item.title: Brazil's Chamber Speaker Commits to AI Law Vote by Late June — Rapporteur Opinion June 9
item.description: Chamber of Deputies President Hugo Motta confirmed the special committee will deliver the rapporteur's final opinion by June 9 and the Plenary will vote before the July 18 recess. Rapporteur Deputy Aguinaldo Ribeiro (PP-PB) finalises the text of PL 2338/2023, which would establish Brazil's first comprehensive AI law with risk-tiered classification, ANPD enforcement, and fines up to R$50 million per infraction.

item.title: Mistral AI Breaks Ground on €1.2B Swedish AI Datacenter with EcoDataCenter in Borlänge
item.description: Mistral AI and EcoDataCenter formally broke ground on a €1.2 billion AI datacenter facility in Borlänge, Sweden — Mistral's first AI infrastructure investment outside France. The facility will host NVIDIA Vera Rubin GPUs and is scheduled to open in 2027, making Sweden one of Europe's largest recipients of dedicated AI infrastructure investment from a European AI lab.

item.title: OpenAI GPT-5.5 and Codex Now Generally Available on Amazon Bedrock
item.description: OpenAI and AWS made GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and Codex generally available on Amazon Bedrock on June 1, 2026, following an April limited preview. Pricing matches OpenAI first-party rates with no separate seat licences; all usage counts toward existing AWS cloud spending commitments. Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI also launched in limited preview.

item.title: OpenAI Expands Codex with Sites, Annotations, and 62 App Plugins
item.description: On June 3, 2026, OpenAI announced Codex Sites (hosted interactive web apps), Annotations (in-place editing), and six role-specific plugin packs connecting 62 business applications including Snowflake, Figma, and Salesforce. Non-developers now represent 20% of Codex's 5 million weekly users and are adopting the platform three times faster than engineers.

item.title: Anthropic Expands Claude Mythos to Critical Infrastructure in 15+ Countries
item.description: Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing on June 2, 2026, extending Claude Mythos Preview access to approximately 150 new organisations across 15+ countries including Japan, South Korea, UK, France, Germany, Canada, and India. Participating organisations have already identified over 10,000 high- and critical-severity vulnerabilities. Samsung, SK Hynix, SK Telecom, Okta, NATO, and ENISA are among the new entrants.

item.title: EU AI Office's GPAI Model Enforcement Powers Activate August 2, 2026
item.description: The EU AI Office confirmed that full enforcement powers for General-Purpose AI model obligations under the AI Act take effect on August 2, 2026. From that date the Commission can compel documentation, order model evaluations, and levy fines of up to €15 million or 3% of global revenue. All new models face immediate obligations; providers of models placed on the market before August 2025 have a grace period to August 2027.

item.title: Brazil AI Bill Faces August Deadline as Camara Committee Nears Vote
item.description: Brazil's AI legislation (PL 2338/2023), already approved by the Senate, is under review in the Câmara dos Deputados where relator Aguinaldo Ribeiro is finalising the report. Analysts expect a committee vote before the August recess, but two unresolved issues — copyright liability for AI training data and the definition of high-risk systems — remain contentious. With October 2026 elections approaching, the legislative window is shrinking rapidly.

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro Enters Limited Vertex Preview with 2M-Token Context and Deep Think Reasoning
item.description: Google opened Gemini 3.5 Pro to select Vertex AI enterprise customers in limited preview in early June 2026, ahead of a broader GA Sundar Pichai described as 'next month' at Google I/O. The model targets a 2-million-token context window, Deep Think multi-step reasoning, and frontier multimodal understanding — absorbing the use cases previously routed to Gemini Ultra.

item.title: EU Cloud and AI Development Act Text Released, Mandates Compute Reporting for Hyperscalers
item.description: The European Commission released the provisional text of the EU Cloud and AI Development Act in early June 2026, requiring hyperscalers operating in the EU to report AI compute capacity to the AI Office quarterly, share excess compute with European academic institutions at below-market rates, and submit AI-training energy consumption data. The Act pairs with the AI Act's GPAI obligations ahead of the August 2026 enforcement date.

item.title: GPT-Rosalind Update Opens Global Research Preview, Cuts Genomics Compute 31%
item.description: OpenAI published a substantial update to GPT-Rosalind on June 3, 2026, opening the research preview to eligible organisations worldwide for the first time. Three new evaluations show the updated model outperforms GPT-5.5 across all tested drug-discovery, genomics, and wet-lab domains while using 31% fewer tokens in genomics analyses. The Rosalind Biodefense programme simultaneously began onboarding vetted US government agencies.

item.title: OpenAI Rolls Out 'Dreaming V3' Hierarchical Memory Architecture to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI began deploying Dreaming V3, a hierarchical background-synthesis memory system, to ChatGPT Plus and Pro users in the US on June 4, 2026. The architecture replaces the saved-memories list, running a background process that continuously synthesises context across conversations. A 5× reduction in compute cost enables the rollout to extend memory capabilities to Free-tier users for the first time.

item.title: Supabase Raises $500M Series F at $10.5B Valuation on 600% Database Growth from AI Agents
item.description: Supabase closed a $500 million Series F round led by GIC on June 4, 2026, valuing the open-source Postgres platform at $10.5 billion — doubling its $5B Series E from October 2025. The company cited 600% year-over-year growth in databases driven primarily by AI coding agents, with Claude Code named as the single largest contributor. Investors include Stripe (second cheque), Salesforce Ventures, Accel, Y Combinator, and Coatue.

item.title: Brain-Inspired AI Startup Flourish Raises $500M at $2.5B Valuation Backed by Bezos
item.description: Flourish, a startup developing brain-inspired AI models targeting 20-watt energy budgets versus ~600 watts for a modern GPU chip, raised $500 million at a $2.5 billion valuation, closing around June 4, 2026. Backers include Jeff Bezos (anchoring with ~$100M), GV (Alphabet's venture arm), Lux Capital, and Catalio Capital. The company was co-founded by Thomas Reardon (creator of Internet Explorer, CTRL-labs founder) and former Amazon S-team executive Rob Williams.

item.title: AI Finance Platform Ramp Raises $750M Series F at $44B Valuation
item.description: AI-powered finance platform Ramp closed a $750 million Series F led by ICONIQ, GIC, and Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, valuing the company at $44 billion as of June 4, 2026 — the most valuable private fintech in the US. Ramp reported ~170% year-over-year growth in total payment volume in March 2026, its highest rate in three years. New investors include Goldman Sachs Alternatives, D.E. Shaw, and Morgan Stanley Investment Management.

item.title: OpenAI Extends GPT-5.5-Cyber to EU Defenders Under New EU Cyber Action Plan
item.description: OpenAI began limited-preview access of GPT-5.5-Cyber to vetted European organisations — including EU institutions, national cyber agencies, and governments — under a newly announced EU Cyber Action Plan. The cybersecurity-tuned model is more permissive for authorised security tasks such as vulnerability analysis, malware reverse-engineering, and patch validation. Advanced Account Security became mandatory for API members with trusted access from June 1, 2026.

item.title: EU Appoints 60-Expert Scientific Panel to Enforce AI Act Ahead of August Deadline
item.description: The European Commission formally appointed a 60-member Scientific Panel and a 174-member Advisory Forum on June 2, 2026 to support enforcement of the EU AI Act. The Scientific Panel — composed of leading independent experts in frontier AI, engineering, and technical auditing — will focus on GPAI model classification, systemic-risk assessment, evaluation methodologies, and cross-border market surveillance. Most AI Act rules apply from August 2, 2026.

item.title: France Secures €93 Billion at Choose France Summit — SoftBank Pledges €75B AI Data Centre Buildout
item.description: At the Choose France summit at Versailles on June 1, President Macron announced €93 billion in foreign investment pledges across 71 projects expected to create 15,600+ jobs. SoftBank committed up to €75 billion to build 5 GW of AI data-centre capacity in France by 2031 — the single largest AI infrastructure pledge in European history — with Phase 1 delivering 3.1 GW in the Hauts-de-France region. Additional AI commitments came from Brookfield ($10B data centre in Escaudain) and other infrastructure investors.

item.title: Germany's SPRIND Closes Applications for €125M Next Frontier AI Challenge to Build European Foundation-Model Labs
item.description: Germany's Federal Agency for Breakthrough Innovation (SPRIND) closed applications on June 1 for the Next Frontier AI challenge — a €125 million, non-dilutive DARPA-style competition to fund at least three European frontier AI labs. Up to 10 teams will receive up to €27 million each across three progressive stages; jury pitches are scheduled June 24–25, with Stage 1 funding beginning in July 2026. Winners are positioned for €1 billion follow-on rounds in 2028.

item.title: Trump Signs AI Executive Order — Voluntary 30-Day Pre-Release Federal Security Review for Frontier Models
item.description: President Trump signed 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security' on June 2, inviting AI developers to voluntarily submit frontier models for up to 30 days of pre-release federal security review. The order directs agencies to develop AI cyber-capability benchmarks, establish an AI cybersecurity clearinghouse for vulnerability coordination, and strengthen government defences. The signing followed a May 21 postponement after Trump sought revisions to restrict mandatory oversight provisions.

item.title: Microsoft Unveils Full MAI Model Family at Build 2026 — Seven Proprietary Models Reduce OpenAI Dependency
item.description: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman introduced seven MAI models at Build 2026 to reduce reliance on OpenAI APIs. The family includes MAI-Thinking-1 (sparse ~1T-parameter MoE reasoning model, trained entirely on commercially licensed data), MAI-Code-1-Flash (5B, integrating into VS Code and GitHub Copilot), MAI-Image-2.5, MAI-Transcribe-1.5, and MAI-Voice-2 (15 languages). All models are available through Azure AI Foundry and integrated across Microsoft 365 Copilot Wave 4.

item.title: WeRide, Uber, and AVOMO Launch Spain's First Commercial Robotaxi Pilot in Madrid
item.description: WeRide, Uber, and Madrid fleet partner AVOMO announced Spain's first commercial robotaxi pilot, with rides available through the Uber app in Madrid's urban core. The service will initially include safety operators before scaling to fully driverless operation. Madrid is the fourth of 15 cities targeted under the WeRide–Uber global agreement, which aims to expand across Europe, the Middle East, and Asia by 2030.

item.title: NeurIPS 2026 Desk-Rejects 178 Papers via AI Detector — False Positive Backlash Erupts
item.description: The NeurIPS 2026 Position Paper Track ran 969 submissions through Pangram v3.3.2, desk-rejecting 178 papers and requiring 123 more to prove human authorship by June 15. Researchers — including track chairs whose own papers scored 24–69% AI-probability — reported that heavy human editing still triggered high false-positive scores. The controversy reignited debate about whether AI-detection tools meet the reliability threshold for consequential academic gatekeeping.

item.title: EU AI Office Closes Article 50 Transparency Consultation — Synthetic Content Labelling Rules Take Effect August 2
item.description: The European Commission's AI Office closed its public consultation on draft Article 50 transparency guidelines on June 3, ending a 26-day comment period. The 40-page draft mandates machine-readable labelling of synthetic audio, images, video, and text, and requires deployers to disclose emotion recognition and biometric categorisation. All obligations take effect August 2, 2026 — the Digital Omnibus did not defer Article 50, making these the AI Act's first enforcement-ready transparency rules.

item.title: EU Proposes Chips Act 2.0 — €120B Semiconductor Investment Targeting AI Chip Leadership by 2030
item.description: The European Commission formally proposed Chips Act 2.0 on June 3 as part of its Tech Sovereignty Package, targeting €120 billion in semiconductor investment by 2030 with an explicit focus on AI chips — projected to account for 70% of the €1.37 trillion global chip market by 2030. The proposal introduces 'Grand Challenges' for AI chip development, accelerates permitting to a 12-month maximum, and enables State aid for first-of-kind projects across the full semiconductor value chain.

item.title: Nvidia Releases Nemotron 3 Ultra — 550B-Parameter Open-Weights MoE, Largest US Open Model
item.description: Nvidia released Nemotron 3 Ultra, a 550B-parameter (55B active) hybrid Mamba-Transformer Mixture-of-Experts model under the permissive OpenMDW-1.1 license — the largest open-weights model from a US lab to date. It scores 48 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and achieves 300+ tokens/second on optimised infrastructure. Weights are available on Hugging Face, OpenRouter, ModelScope, and NVIDIA NIM.

item.title: Canada Launches 'AI for All' National Strategy with C$2B+ Investment — 90,000 Jobs Target
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled Canada's 'AI for All' national strategy targeting 90,000 AI-related jobs and placements, a public AI supercomputer, a National AI Literacy Initiative with free entry-level training, and AI agent access for every post-secondary student. New legislation on data protection and an online safety regime was included. Only 12% of Canadian businesses currently use AI in production — one of the lowest rates in the G7.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Roadshow Begins — S-1 Reveals Anthropic Paying $1.25B/Month for Colossus GPU Cluster
item.description: SpaceX began its IPO investor roadshow June 4–8, targeting a Nasdaq listing under SPCX around June 12 and aiming to raise up to $75 billion. Its S-1 disclosed that Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25 billion per month through May 2029 for 300 megawatts of Colossus capacity (~220,000+ NVIDIA GPUs) — a $45B+ total contract. The S-1 also reported $7.7 billion in Q1 2026 capex and a $2.4 billion operating loss.

item.title: Google Signs $32B Compute Deal with SpaceX at $920M/Month for 110,000 Nvidia GPUs
item.description: Google agreed to pay SpaceX $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029 — approximately $32 billion total — for access to roughly 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs at xAI's Colossus data centres. Google Cloud cited surging demand for Gemini Enterprise as the driver. Combined with Anthropic's $1.25B/month agreement, xAI's Colossus infrastructure now receives $2.175B/month from rival AI labs.

item.title: Mistral AI Now Summit: Vibe Assistant, Industrial AI Platform, and €4B Compute Expansion Announced
item.description: At its inaugural AI Now Summit in Paris on May 28, Mistral announced the rebrand of Le Chat to Vibe — a broader organisational AI assistant targeting email, documents, and code workflows — alongside Mistral for Industrial Engineering, an integrated AI-plus-physics-simulation stack built partly on the acquisition of Emmi AI. The company disclosed 1,000 employees, a €1 billion 2026 revenue target, and a new 10 MW Les Ulis inference data centre (Q3 2026) powered by NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 accelerators. Mistral Compute, its infrastructure arm, targets 200 MW by 2027 and 1 GW by 2030 as part of a €4 billion programme in France and Sweden.

item.title: Bpifrance, Mistral, and MGX Expand Campus AI to 3 GW Nationwide — Second French Site Selected
item.description: On June 2, the Campus AI joint venture — comprising Bpifrance, Mistral AI, MGX (Abu Dhabi), and NVIDIA — announced the imminent selection of a second French site, doubling its initial investment and targeting 3 GW of AI compute capacity across a pan-European network of AI factories. The first flagship campus at Fouju in Seine-et-Marne is already underway; construction at the second site is scheduled to begin H2 2026, with operations targeted for 2028. The expansion reinforces France's position as Europe's primary destination for large-scale, decarbonised AI compute.

item.title: Poland PM Tusk Announces 'Sovereignty Test' for Government Tech Purchases at European Financial Congress
item.description: At the European Financial Congress in Sopot on June 2, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced that Poland will introduce a mandatory 'sovereignty test' for significant state purchases of technology, requiring annual public reports on IT independence progress. Tusk warned that Poland's dependency on a narrow group of foreign technology suppliers had reached dangerous proportions and cited France, Denmark, and Germany as models to emulate. The government simultaneously confirmed a nearly €500 million allocation — mostly from EU funds — to deploy AI laboratories in 12,000 Polish schools.

item.title: EU €20B AI Gigafactory Programme Stumbles — Bids Drop from 70 to ~10, Funding Round Pushed to July
item.description: Bloomberg reported on June 2 that the EU's €20 billion InvestAI gigafactory programme — designed to build at least five 100,000-chip, 1 GW AI data centres — is facing a severe credibility crisis. The bidding round originally scheduled for May was deferred to July, and only two of the five centres can receive subsidy disbursements before the EU's 2028 budget cycle; interest from potential bidders has shrunk from 70 companies to roughly 10, with at least two consortia reconsidering entirely. Schwarz Group and Deutsche Telekom have dampened enthusiasm and are proceeding with independent data centre investments instead.
